artifact: doc/ — 24 specification, research, and architecture documents
chairman_summary: The dart_quic specification corpus PASSES with a strong weighted score of 7.95. This is a comprehensive, well-structured documentation set that fills a genuine ecosystem gap (pure-Dart QUIC) and positions the project for systematic implementation. Capability and Evolution scored highest (9 each), reflecting the strategic value and completeness of coverage across QUIC, HTTP/3, WebTransport, and libp2p. Safety scored well (8) — the corpus correctly specifies security boundaries without introducing any risk. Efficiency (6) is the lowest but still passing — some duplication exists between research and spec layers, which is an intentional design choice for standalone readability. The pivotal lens is Evolution: this corpus is catalytic for the Dart networking ecosystem.

findings.finding: Missing doc/INDEX.md — a navigational index would improve discoverability across 24 documents
findings.recommendation: Create a top-level INDEX.md with document descriptions and reading order

findings.finding: RFC 9221 (QUIC Datagrams) lacks standalone specification — covered only within WEBTRANSPORT_SPEC.md
findings.recommendation: Consider a brief QUIC_DATAGRAM_SPEC.md for non-WebTransport datagram use cases

findings.finding: Cross-references use file names ('QUIC_WIRE_SPEC.md') rather than relative links
findings.recommendation: Use relative markdown links for better tooling support: [Wire Spec](../specs/QUIC_WIRE_SPEC.md)

findings.finding: DATA_FLOW.md ASCII art boxes are verbose (~8 lines each where 3-4 would convey the same information)
findings.recommendation: Consider condensing processing pipeline steps for readability

findings.finding: No explicit certificate revocation (CRL/OCSP) guidance in SECURITY_SPEC.md
findings.recommendation: Add a section on revocation strategy (even if deferred to implementation phase)
Add a brief note in ROADMAP.md acknowledging QUICv2 as a future extension

action_items: Create doc/INDEX.md as a navigational guide
Add CRL/OCSP section to SECURITY_SPEC.md (can be brief/deferred)
Add QUICv2 mention to ROADMAP.md future extensions
